_comment: Settings diff for ~/.claude/settings.json. These are the minimal additions to wire up the debounced continuity guard and expose AUTONOMOUS_MODE to hooks. Paste the 'hooks.Stop' addition into your existing Stop array and add the env var to the top level if desired.
_diff_intent: Replace the current Stop-hook circuit-engine nag path (if any) with the debounced guard script. If the user's current setup does NOT have a Stop-hook guard (the audit could not find one in settings.json — the nag may be coming from circuit-engine itself via SessionStart stop_hook_feedback injection), skip this and instead fix it inside the circuit-engine project.

env._comment: Optional: set AUTONOMOUS_MODE=1 in a shell before starting an overnight session and the guard self-suppresses.

_notes: The current settings.json has Stop hooks firing hud-state-tracker.sh + capacitor webhooks. The continuity nag appears to be emitted by circuit-engine itself (as stop_hook_feedback in its event model), not via settings.json. To fully fix, you may need to patch the circuit-engine project to consult the same debounce state file. This drafted hook works standalone if you add a SessionStart hook in circuit-engine that forwards guard evaluation to this script instead of emitting the nag itself.
Environment variable CONTINUITY_GUARD_DEBOUNCE controls the per-session cooldown (default 600s).
Environment variable CONTINUITY_GUARD_MAX_NAGS caps total nags per session (default 2).
